data.name: content
data.title: Heading (CMS) Harry Potter locations itinerary
data.teaser: Teaser (CMS) : It was in Scotland's capital city that JK Rowling started to plot the adventures of Harry and his friends

introduction.value:
Introduction (CMS)

It was in Scotland's capital city that JK Rowling started to plot the adventures of Harry and his friends. With cobbled streets, winding alleys and medieval architecture, the city certainly have a few things in common with the settings from the books and films. She wrote the early chapters in various cafés around the city's Old Town, before coming back to finish off the series from a peaceful suite in The Balmoral Hotel. It is also said that Hogwarts School was styled on Edinburgh’s Fettes College, a private school on the outskirts of Edinburgh’s west end new town. You could also try the Wizarding World of Edinburgh tour.

data.seoTitle: Iconic Harry Potter Film Locations - An Itinerary
data.seoDescription: It was in Scotland's capital city that JK Rowling started to plot the adventures

heroImage.altText: Castle on a volcanic plug or something

introduction.value: It was in Scotland's capital city that JK Rowling started to plot the adventures of Harry and his friends. With cobbled streets, winding alleys and medieval architecture, the city certainly has a few things in common with the settings from the books and films. She wrote the early chapters in various cafés around the city's Old Town, before coming back to finish off the series from a peaceful suite in The Balmoral Hotel. It is also said that Hogwarts School was styled on Edinburgh’s Fettes College, a private school on the outskirts of Edinburgh’s west end new town. You could also try the Wizarding World of Edinburgh tour.

errorMessages: The DMS product added to 'Edinburgh castle' was not found, please review the DMS Product id field at: /content/documents/visitscotland/sandbox/shared/itinerary-stops/edinburgh-castle/edinburgh-castle/visitscotland:product
stops.title: Edinburgh castle

description.value: Make Edinburgh Castle your first port of call. It sits at the top of the historic Royal Mile on a volcanic plug, and is perhaps the most famous landmark on the Edinburgh skyline. Beat the crowds and get there for opening at 9.30am. The views over the city are pretty spectacular, and there is so much to take in, including the National Museum of War, the National War Monument and the Honours of Scotland, the county's Crown Jewels.

tipsBody.value: Edinburgh Castle is now open and we are delighted to welcome you back. Find out more about our re-opening plans.

errorMessages: The DMS product added to 'Old Town' was not found, please review the DMS Product id field at: /content/documents/visitscotland/sandbox/shared/itinerary-stops/old-town/old-town/visitscotland:product
stops.title: Old Town

description.value:
Edinburgh's Old Town is part of a UNESCO World Heritage Site, together with the Georgian era New Town on the north side of the city centre. The buildings you’ll see remain largely unchanged from how they would have looked centuries ago.

The Old Town and Royal Mile is home to three filming locations from Outlander. If you’re looking for a memorable souvenir while you’re there, pop into Hamilton & Young Jewellery Designers on the Royal Mile to browse Celtic and Outlander inspired items.

errorMessages: The DMS product added to 'Greyfriars Kirkyard' was not found, please review the DMS Product id field at: /content/documents/visitscotland/sandbox/shared/itinerary-stops/greyfriars-kirkyard/greyfriars-kirkyard/visitscotland:product
stops.title: Greyfriars Kirkyard

description.value: Only a short walk away from The Elephant House you'll find this infamous, spine-tingling graveyard, and burial place of several notable Edinburgh academics and writers of the past. However, also buried here are a few other notable residents: Thomas Riddell, William McGonagall and Elizabeth Moodie. A coincidence? We think not.

errorMessages: The DMS product added to 'Palace of Holyroodhouse' was not found, please review the DMS Product id field at: /content/documents/visitscotland/sandbox/shared/itinerary-stops/palace-of-holyroodhouse/palace-of-holyroodhouse/visitscotland:product
stops.title: Palace of Holyroodhouse

description.value:
Book location and Jacobite connection

Located at the foot of the Royal Mile, the Palace of Holyroodhouse is an elegant royal residence with links to monarchs from throughout the centuries. Today it is the official Scottish residence of Her Majesty The Queen.

In September 1745 Bonnie Prince Charlie established his court at Holyroodhouse for six weeks. This is portrayed in the Outlander novels when Claire and Jamie visit the Prince at the palace and beg him to abandon his hopeless cause.

introduction.value:
If 'apparating' isn't an option for you, it's possible to travel by train from Edinburgh to Glasgow and from there, take the West Highland Line to Fort William. This is a great way to see some famous spots from the window, including the mystical Rannoch Moor, the place where Death Eaters board the train in the movie Harry Potter and The Deathly Hallows: Part 1. 

If you'd prefer to drive, you could also hire a car in Edinburgh. Drive to the Bo'ness Motor Museum, where you can see Ron Weasley's Ford Anglia or drop in to the Scottish Owl Centre in West Lothian to say hello to some of Hedwig's friends and other exotic owl species.

errorMessages: The DMS product added to 'Linlithgow Palace' was not found, please review the DMS Product id field at: /content/documents/visitscotland/sandbox/shared/itinerary-stops/linlithgow-palace/linlithgow-palace/visitscotland:product
stops.title: Linlithgow Palace

description.value:
Jacobite connection and filming location – season 1

Explore the nooks and towers of this sizeable ruined, yet still beautiful palace. The various floors are still accessible via the intact turret staircases, and beautiful views can be observed from the roof. In 1745 Bonnie Prince Charlie visited the palace on his journey south. Its elaborate courtyard fountain is said to have flowed with red wine to mark the occasion! In Outlander, the majestic entrance and corridors feature as Wentworth Prison where Jamie was imprisoned.

You could also take a refreshing walk around Linlithgow Loch, the shores of which the palace sits upon.

Linlithgow has a direct rail connection to Edinburgh which takes just 20 minutes.

Stay in Edinburgh or Linlithgow.

tipsBody.value: The majestic ruins of Linlithgow Palace stands in for the prison entrance and corridors in the scene where Jamie was imprisoned.

errorMessages: The DMS product added to 'Drum Castle' was not found, please review the DMS Product id field at: /content/documents/visitscotland/sandbox/shared/itinerary-stops/drum-castle/drum-castle/visitscotland:product
stops.title: Drum Castle

description.value: Begin the trail at the spectacular Drum Castle, located in Drumoak just outside Aberdeen, which combines a 13th century tower, a Jacobean mansion house and Victorian architecture. Take a tour of the castle and marvel at the fascinating combination of Scottish history and Victorian fashion, and wander the grounds and the magnificent historic rose garden.

image.description: Man looking forward to finish this itinerary

errorMessages: The DMS product added to 'Crathes Castle' was not found, please review the DMS Product id field at: /content/documents/visitscotland/sandbox/shared/itinerary-stops/crathes-castle/crathes-castle/visitscotland:product
stops.title: Crathes Castle

description.value: Pick up the trail again and head on a five minute drive to the spooky Crathes Castle, a favourite place of Queen Victoria and one where she often stayed. Explore the wonderland grounds and marvel at the ornate gargoyles and fairytale turrets. On your tour of the castle you'll learn about the castle's intriguing history along with the story of its resident ghosts, the Green Lady and her infant child. It is even said that Queen Victoria witnessed the ghostly pair herself.

The Old Town and Royal Mile is home to three filming locations from Outlander. If you’re looking for a memorable souvenir while you’re there, pop into Hamilton & Young Jewellery Designers on the Royal Mile to browse Celtic and Outlander inspired items.

data.title: Explore VS listicle examples in our Sandbox area
data.teaser: If you're looking for the best things to do in Scotland, then look no further. We've got iconic castles, incredible museums.

introduction.value: Take a look below at the top 20 and see how many you've visited and make a plan to tick the others off your list. Plus, what else would you recommend? Hop over to our iKnow Scotland Community to share your tips and pick up a couple more for your next break or day out in Scotland.

introduction.value: Lorem Ipsum is simply dummy text of the printing and typesetting industry. Lorem Ipsum has been the industry's standard dummy text ever since the 1500s, when an unknown printer took a galley of type and scrambled it to make a type specimen book. It has survived not only five centuries, but also the leap into electronic typesetting, remaining essentially unchanged. It was popularised in the 1960s with the release of Letraset sheets containing Lorem Ipsum passage

data.name: content
data.displayName: One Day in Inverness - A 24hr Itinerary
data.title: Experience Inverness in One Day
data.teaser: Experience some of the best attractions Inverness has to offer with this one day itinerary.

introduction.value: Small but mighty, Inverness is quickly becoming a popular city break destination for visitors from around the world. Experience some of the best attractions Inverness has to offer with this one day itinerary. From art galleries and museums to food & drink, Inverness has it all!

data.seoTitle: One Day in Inverness - A 24hr Itinerary
data.seoDescription: Experience the best of Inverness in one-day with a fun-filled itinerary from VisitScotland. From a historic battlefield to dining out, plan a day trip now.

stops.title: Linlithgow Palace

description.value:
Jacobite connection and filming location – season 1

Explore the nooks and towers of this sizeable ruined, yet still beautiful palace. The various floors are still accessible via the intact turret staircases, and beautiful views can be observed from the roof. In 1745 Bonnie Prince Charlie visited the palace on his journey south. Its elaborate courtyard fountain is said to have flowed with red wine to mark the occasion! In Outlander, the majestic entrance and corridors feature as Wentworth Prison where Jamie was imprisoned.

You could also take a refreshing walk around Linlithgow Loch, the shores of which the palace sits upon.

Linlithgow has a direct rail connection to Edinburgh which takes just 20 minutes.

Stay in Edinburgh or Linlithgow.

tipsBody.value: The majestic ruins of Linlithgow Palace stands in for the prison entrance and corridors in the scene where Jamie was imprisoned.

stops.title: Drum Castle

description.value: Begin the trail at the spectacular Drum Castle, located in Drumoak just outside Aberdeen, which combines a 13th century tower, a Jacobean mansion house and Victorian architecture. Take a tour of the castle and marvel at the fascinating combination of Scottish history and Victorian fashion, and wander the grounds and the magnificent historic rose garden.

errorMessages: The DMS product added to 'Crathes Castle' was not found, please review the DMS Product id field at: /content/documents/visitscotland/sandbox/shared/itinerary-stops/crathes-castle/crathes-castle/visitscotland:product
stops.title: Crathes Castle

description.value: Pick up the trail again and head on a five minute drive to the spooky Crathes Castle, a favourite place of Queen Victoria and one where she often stayed. Explore the wonderland grounds and marvel at the ornate gargoyles and fairytale turrets. On your tour of the castle you'll learn about the castle's intriguing history along with the story of its resident ghosts, the Green Lady and her infant child. It is even said that Queen Victoria witnessed the ghostly pair herself.

Edinburgh's Old Town is part of a UNESCO World Heritage Site, together with the Georgian era New Town on the north side of the city centre. The buildings you’ll see remain largely unchanged from how they would have looked centuries ago.

The Old Town and Royal Mile is home to three filming locations from Outlander. If you’re looking for a memorable souvenir while you’re there, pop into Hamilton & Young Jewellery Designers on the Royal Mile to browse Celtic and Outlander inspired items.

Only a short walk away from The Elephant House you'll find this infamous, spine-tingling graveyard, and burial place of several notable Edinburgh academics and writers of the past. However, also buried here are a few other notable residents: Thomas Riddell, William McGonagall and Elizabeth Moodie. A coincidence? We think not.

Begin the trail at the spectacular Drum Castle, located in Drumoak just outside Aberdeen, which combines a 13th century tower, a Jacobean mansion house and Victorian architecture. Take a tour of the castle and marvel at the fascinating combination of Scottish history and Victorian fashion, and wander the grounds and the magnificent historic rose garden.

description.value:
Book location and Jacobite connection

Located at the foot of the Royal Mile, the Palace of Holyroodhouse is an elegant royal residence with links to monarchs from throughout the centuries. Today it is the official Scottish residence of Her Majesty The Queen.

In September 1745 Bonnie Prince Charlie established his court at Holyroodhouse for six weeks. This is portrayed in the Outlander novels when Claire and Jamie visit the Prince at the palace and beg him to abandon his hopeless cause.

Pick up the trail again and head on a five minute drive to the spooky Crathes Castle, a favourite place of Queen Victoria and one where she often stayed. Explore the wonderland grounds and marvel at the ornate gargoyles and fairytale turrets. On your tour of the castle you'll learn about the castle's intriguing history along with the story of its resident ghosts, the Green Lady and her infant child. It is even said that Queen Victoria witnessed the ghostly pair herself.

Jacobite connection and filming location – season 1

Explore the nooks and towers of this sizeable ruined, yet still beautiful palace. The various floors are still accessible via the intact turret staircases, and beautiful views can be observed from the roof. In 1745 Bonnie Prince Charlie visited the palace on his journey south. Its elaborate courtyard fountain is said to have flowed with red wine to mark the occasion! In Outlander, the majestic entrance and corridors feature as Wentworth Prison where Jamie was imprisoned.

You could also take a refreshing walk around Linlithgow Loch, the shores of which the palace sits upon.

Linlithgow has a direct rail connection to Edinburgh which takes just 20 minutes.

Stay in Edinburgh or Linlithgow.

description.value: Make Edinburgh Castle your first port of call. It sits at the top of the historic Royal Mile on a volcanic plug, and is perhaps the most famous landmark on the Edinburgh skyline. Beat the crowds and get there for opening at 9.30am. The views over the city are pretty spectacular, and there is so much to take in, including the National Museum of War, the National War Monument and the Honours of Scotland, the county's Crown Jewels.

copy.value: Edinburgh Castle is now open and we are delighted to welcome you back. Find out more about our re-opening plans.
